name: kafka-stream-split-trigger
title: Split Kafka Stream
description: Consumes messages from a Kafka topic and routes each message to one or more handler branches based on content-based predicates — stream-split / content-based routing pattern. Supports first-match (switch/case) and all-match (fan-out) routing modes, multi-predicate AND/OR evaluation, regex matching, catch-all unmatched branches, error DLQ branches, and tap (monitor-all) branches.

display.description: Route each Kafka message to one or more handler branches based on content-based predicates.

display.name: Kafka Connection
display.description: TIBCO Kafka shared connection providing broker addresses, authentication, and TLS settings.

display.name: Source Topic
display.description: Kafka topic to consume messages from and split into sub-streams.

display.name: Consumer Group
display.description: Kafka consumer group ID. Each trigger instance in the same group shares partition load.

display.name: Initial Offset
display.description: Where to start consuming when no committed offset exists for this consumer group.

display.name: Routing Mode
display.description: 'first-match' (default): evaluate handlers in priority order; the FIRST handler whose predicates pass receives the message — exclusive routing, like a switch/case chain. 'all-match': evaluate ALL handlers; every handler whose predicates pass receives a copy of the message — fan-out routing.

display.name: Balance Strategy
display.description: Kafka consumer group rebalance strategy. 'roundrobin' (default) — simple round-robin partition assignment. 'sticky' — minimises partition reassignments on rebalance. 'range' — assigns contiguous partition ranges per consumer.

display.name: Commit Offset on Success Only
display.description: When true (default), the Kafka offset is committed only when all fired handlers complete without error — the message is redelivered on flow failure (at-least-once). When false, the offset is always committed regardless of handler result (at-most-once).

display.name: Handler Timeout (ms)
display.description: Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for all handlers to complete for a single message. 0 (default) means no timeout. When exceeded the handler is treated as failed and, if Commit Offset on Success Only is true, the offset is not marked.

display.name: Event Type
display.description: 'matched' (default): fires when this handler's predicates match the message — define Field/Predicates below. 'unmatched': fires when NO matched handler's predicates passed — catch-all / DLQ branch, no predicates needed. 'evalError': fires when predicate evaluation fails — error DLQ, no predicates needed. 'all': fires for every message regardless of routing outcome — tap/monitor/audit, no predicates needed.

display.name: Priority
display.description: Evaluation order for 'first-match' routing mode. Handlers with lower Priority values are evaluated first. Handlers with equal Priority are evaluated in registration order. Has no effect for 'all-match' mode or for unmatched/evalError/all event types.

display.name: Field
display.description: Message field to evaluate in single-predicate mode. Leave empty when using multi-predicate mode (Predicates JSON). Only applies when Event Type is 'matched'.

display.name: Operator
display.description: Comparison operator for single-predicate mode. Only applies when Event Type is 'matched'.

display.name: Value
display.description: Comparison value for single-predicate mode. Only applies when Event Type is 'matched'.

display.name: Predicates (JSON)
display.description: Multi-predicate definition as a JSON array. E.g. [{"field":"status","operator":"eq","value":"ok"},{"field":"region","operator":"eq","value":"us-east"}]. Evaluated using Predicate Mode (AND/OR). Only applies when Event Type is 'matched'.

display.name: Predicate Mode
display.description: How multiple predicates are combined. 'and' (default): all predicates must pass. 'or': at least one must pass. Only applies when Event Type is 'matched' and Predicates JSON is set.
